strategy: Stabilize what is already implemented, keep the user contract explicit, replace the transpose shortcut with a proper structural view, and expose full GEMM control explicitly before another broad expansion of algorithm families.

sequence.title: Harden am_rsvd precision contract
sequence.objective: Make standalone am_rsvd() obey the same fast-only backend rule as am_svd_factor(), and keep the broad conformance slice green under strict semantics.
scope: Dispatch backend rsvd only when x is an adgeMatrix in fast precision
Keep strict precision on the CPU reference path
Move stochastic fast-path numeric expectations out of the broad cross-backend conformance harness and into focused backend-local or dispatch tests
acceptance_criteria: Broad backend-integration, conformance, and cross-backend-conformance slices pass
Strict precision no longer calls float32-only backend rsvd paths
Standalone am_rsvd() has direct dispatch tests for both strict and fast precision

sequence.title: Freeze public mode and backend contract
sequence.objective: Stop promising behavior the package does not yet implement and make the current contract explicit everywhere users will read it.
scope: Document exact as strict CPU semantics
Document balanced conservatively as strict CPU behavior unless an explicit backend is supplied
Document fast plus backend as the current accelerator opt-in
Align README, PRD, flagship-workflows, and kernel-model-core docs
acceptance_criteria: No core doc claims balanced automatic GPU routing as a shipped behavior
The mode/backend story is consistent across README and docs
Strict-versus-fast semantics are explicit for decomposition paths

sequence.title: Validate MLX GPU Cholesky plus batched solve on flagship SPD workflows
sequence.objective: Turn the existing MLX bridge-backed Cholesky and triangular-solve path into a benchmarked, trustworthy second flagship.
scope: Add focused parity tests around am_chol_factor() and am_chol_solve() in fast MLX mode
Benchmark representative SPD many-RHS workloads such as ridge-like solves or kernel systems
Document where the MLX path is already real and where ArrayFire remains a stub or fallback
acceptance_criteria: MLX factor and solve paths have benchmark-backed validation
Docs describe the Cholesky workflow honestly as implemented and validated, not merely planned
Any remaining backend gaps are explicit

sequence.title: Reduce compact MLX QR cold factor-build cost
sequence.objective: Keep pushing the flagship many-RHS QR workflow where the current bottleneck actually is: cold compact-factor construction, not the hot solve path.
scope: Profile compact QR factor build separately from qr.coef()/many_lm hot helpers
Move more compact-path setup below R if the current cost is bridge/setup dominated
Re-benchmark resident-Q versus compact MLX paths on many_lm
acceptance_criteria: Compact QR cold-build cost is measured and reduced or clearly explained
The docs can state whether compact or resident-Q is the premium QR implementation today
The flagship many-RHS benchmark story is stronger after the pass

sequence.title: Land block Lanczos prototype after flagship validation
sequence.objective: After the flagship QR and Cholesky passes, add and benchmark a first GEMM-oriented block Lanczos surface against the am_irlba compatibility path.
scope: Expose a GEMM-oriented block Lanczos surface in the main package
Benchmark against am_irlba compatibility mode and CPU baselines
Document the current accuracy-versus-speed envelope honestly so the prototype is not mistaken for a strict replacement
acceptance_criteria: Block Lanczos shows a credible benchmark-backed win over the current compatibility path
The current prototype status and error envelope are explicit in the docs
The work does not pre-empt unfinished flagship workflow hardening

sequence.title: Replace the src_id transpose shortcut with a proper structural view and expose explicit GEMM
sequence.objective: Keep ordinary algebra pleasant while staying honest about the eager S4 contract. The current transpose shortcut fixes the GPU re-upload problem, but it still materializes host data; the next step is a real view class plus a public explicit GEMM surface.
scope: Replace the current src_id-linked transposed adgeMatrix shortcut with a dedicated aTransposeView or aMatrixView design
Preserve distinct cache and residency identity from the source matrix while keeping t(A) %*% B and A %*% t(B) on the fast crossprod/tcrossprod paths
Collapse t(t(A)) cleanly without redundant host transposition
Promote internal .am_gemm to a documented public am_gemm(A, B, C = NULL, alpha = 1, beta = 0, transA = FALSE, transB = FALSE) surface
acceptance_criteria: Docs, issue tracker, and implementation plan agree that structural views are allowed while full GEMM control stays explicit
Transpose-heavy hot paths no longer need an O(nm) host transpose once the follow-up lands
No roadmap doc implies that a general lazy expression system is the default plan

sequence.title: Decide product status of ArrayFire rsvd
sequence.objective: Make an explicit decision about whether the current ArrayFire rsvd path is ready to advertise as parity or should remain experimental until better quality/performance data exists.
sequence.rationale: Algorithm correct (standard randomized SVD with power iteration). Bridges use the corrected variants from c21561d. Conformance test always skips (guards on amatrix_arrayfire_is_available() which returns false in CI). No real-hardware quality or performance data vs MLX exists. Recommended path on Apple Silicon is MLX. Users with AF hardware can enable via the rsvd_min_dim threshold.
scope: Measure quality and performance against CPU and MLX references
Document whether the path is product-ready, experimental, or fallback-only
Keep docs and tests aligned with that decision
acceptance_criteria: ArrayFire rsvd status is documented honestly
No roadmap doc still treats ArrayFire rsvd as both 'not implemented' and 'shipped'
